confirmed.note: 53-line verbatim copy of three local lambdas (best_in_range / range_average / parabola_delta) plus their peak-pick/parabola-delta usage block, duplicated across two coarse/fine scan routines in the same file; should have been a file-scope helper. Both blocks added in this commit (new MSK144 worker).

extern-C wrapper ftx_ft8_a7d_ref_c is a copy of ftx_ft8_a7d_c: identical 12-arg out-param signature plus identical null-check + sentinel-init preamble for msg37/nharderrors/dmin/xsnr; only the decode body diverges. Two near-twin Fortran-bridge entry points introduced together in this commit.

false_positives.what_happened: Four numeric Qt property setters added together share the canonical Qt setter idiom: v = qBound(lo,v,hi); if (m_x != v) { m_x = v; emit xChanged(); QSettings().setValue("CallFeature/Key", v); }. Only the clamp bounds and the QSettings key string differ, so a token-level detector sees 4 near-identical 6-7 line blocks as within-file copypaste. This is mandatory NVI/property boilerplate, not extractable duplication.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: add a Qt-property-setter idiom filter — if a flagged block matches the shape <clamp?; if (member != v) { member = v; emit *Changed(); persist(...) }> treat as boilerplate; more generally raise min-window for blocks whose only inter-block differences are (a) a member/method identifier sharing a common prefix and (b) a string literal, and require >=2 differing *statements* (not just literals) before counting as a clone.

false_positives.what_happened: Serial HTTP dispatch: each route handler is if (method==GET && route=="/x") { QByteArray response; body = gen(); response += "HTTP/1.1 200 OK\r\n"; + Content-Type + Cache-Control + Content-Length + Connection; write; disconnectFromHost; return; }. Consecutive route arms differ only in the route string, the body generator, the content-type and the cache header, so the fixed 5-line response-assembly tail repeats verbatim and trips within-file copy detection.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: treat verbatim string-literal-heavy lines (HTTP header constants, response += "...\r\n") as low-entropy and weight them down in the clone score; combined with the existing literal-divergence check, a run whose differing lines are only a route literal + a content-type literal should fall below threshold. Alternatively expose a per-file 'dispatch table' idiom suppression when N>=3 sibling if-arms share the same skeleton.

cycle.desc: no cyclic SCC: archcheck --graph reports nodes=728 edges=1191 sccs_cyclic=0 largest_scc=1
cycle.evidence: Author code is organized as a flat-ish include DAG (Detector/, Network/, Transceiver/, Modulator/, widgets/, DecodiumBridge). No back-edge between any header pair; matches corpus hint (циклы=0, SCC<=1).
